conditions: 6 AM—Pssst, hey. You'd better get up because it's time to get down on an energetic day of midweek storm skiing. Early Tuesday morning on Rendezvous Mountain features a little one-inch topping overnight, and an end to the fresh accumulations is nowhere in sight. Warm high temperatures will reach the mid- to upper 20s at the summit and may produce some liquid precipitation down in the Village as it tickles the upper 30s. Strong west winds in the 10-40 mph range will accelerate the healing on the hill, as every nook and cranny is continually refilled. You can chalk it up to instinct or even some kind of mystical intuition, but whatever you do, listen to the inner voice urging you to spend the day on a personal deep powder mission. -Lege
